slides.title: A New Kind of Array 🔗
slides.content:
A standard array (`string[]`) can have 0 strings, or it can have 10,000 strings.

A **Tuple** is a special feature perfectly suited for when you know *exactly* how many elements the array will hold, and what type *each specific element* is.

slides.title: Tuple Syntax
slides.content: To define a tuple, you specify the exact type for each slot inside the brackets `[]`.

quizData.question: Given `let coord: [number, number];`, which assignment is valid?
quizData.explanation: Because the tuple strictly defines exactly two numbers, providing anything more or less throws an error.

slides.title: Strict Ordering 📏
slides.content:
Tuples are strict about order. 

If you type `[string, number]`, you absolutely cannot assign `[5, "Hello"]`. The types must match the declared order perfectly.

slides.title: CSV Data 📊
slides.content: Tuples are famously useful for reading CSV (Comma Separated Values) data, where every line has the exact same expected structure of types (e.g. ID, Name, Salary).
